inputs.goal: Make one root npm ci install the root package and both buildable components so the unified verification runs directly from a clean worktree.
acceptance: Change Proposal and Impact Analysis precede implementation.
The root manifest and lock contain exactly the YPmcn and vector-mcp workspaces.
One root npm ci is sufficient for npm run verify and npm run pack:yp.
All 173 offline tests, package checks, and tracked secret scan pass.
No formal Spec, component manifest/source, CI, provider, database, credential, or release archive source changes occur.
executor_evidence: The pre-fix root workspace regression test failed because package.json had no workspaces field.
A clean root npm ci installed 313 packages.
npm run verify passed 173 of 173 tests.
npm run pack:yp and the tracked secret scan passed.

commands.expected: pass from a dependency-clean detached worktree

adversarial: Check scope drift against the approved Change Proposal.
Check workspace links and dependency ownership in the generated lockfile.
Check npm hoisting still lets both components build.
Check the regression test fails if either workspace is missing or stale.
Check test inventory arithmetic and documentation consistency.
Check verification leaves tracked files clean and staging absent.

evidence: The proposal and impact analysis were committed before the regression test and implementation commits.
A fresh detached worktree had no node_modules; one root npm ci added 313 packages and exposed TypeScript 5.9.3 to both workspaces.
The independent verifier reran the unified gate: 6 + 1 + 16 + 104 + 8 + 16 + 14 + 8 = 173 tests, all passing.
Both YPmcn and vector-mcp built successfully through the hoisted root installation.
The root lock workspace entries match both component manifests and component lockfiles.
Pack, secret scan, archive hash, staging cleanup, forbidden-path diff, and final tracked status all passed.
